crisis: Displacement from Sudan to South Sudan

tavily.answer: The latest conflict and security developments in South Sudan have led to significant displacement. In 2025, internal displacements caused by violence or conflict reached a record high, surpassing those caused by natural disasters for the first time, according to a report by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre. The United Nations’ food agencies have warned that acute hunger is set to worsen across 13 global hotspots, including South Sudan, due to conflict, funding shortages, and climate shocks. The U.S. has imposed visa restrictions on South Sudanese officials accused of undermining a fragile peace deal and obstructing the ceasefire agreement. The ongoing conflict in Sudan has also contributed to the displacement crisis, with over 14 million people forced to flee their homes.
